{{Short description|Classical mechanics law describing gravitational attraction between masses}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Newton's law of universal gravitation
| image = NewtonsLawOfUniversalGravitation.svg
| caption = Every point mass attracts every other point mass by a force pointing along the line intersecting both points.
| topics = [[Gravitation]], [[Force]], [[Classical mechanics]], [[Kepler's laws of planetary motion]]
| formula = <math>F = G\frac{m_1 m_2}{r^2}</math>
}}

'''Newton's law of universal gravitation''' states that every [[point mass]] attracts every other point mass in the [[universe]] by a [[force]] acting along the line intersecting both points. The force is proportional to the product of the two [[mass]]es and inversely proportional to the square of the [[distance]] between them:

:<math>F = G\frac{m_1 m_2}{r^2}</math>

where:
* <math>F</math> is the gravitational force between two masses
* <math>m_1</math> and <math>m_2</math> are the masses
* <math>r</math> is the distance between the centres of the masses
* <math>G</math> is the [[gravitational constant]], approximately 6.674×10⁻¹¹ N·m²/kg²

This is a general physical law derived from empirical observations by what [[Isaac Newton]] called [[inductive reasoning]]. It is a part of [[classical mechanics]] and was formulated in Newton's work ''[[Philosophiæ Naturalis Principia Mathematica]]'' (1687).

== Vector form ==
In vector notation, the force on mass <math>m_1</math> due to <math>m_2</math> is:
:<math>\mathbf{F}_{12} = -G\frac{m_1 m_2}{|\mathbf{r}_{12}|^2}\hat{\mathbf{r}}_{12}</math>
where <math>\hat{\mathbf{r}}_{12}</math> is the unit vector from <math>m_2</math> to <math>m_1</math>.

== Gravitational field ==
The [[gravitational field]] <math>\mathbf{g}</math> is the force per unit mass:
:<math>\mathbf{g}(\mathbf{r}) = -G\sum_i \frac{m_i}{|\mathbf{r} - \mathbf{r}_i|^2}\hat{\mathbf{r}}_i</math>

For a spherically symmetric body of mass <math>M</math>, the field outside is:
:<math>g = G\frac{M}{r^2}</math>

== Relation to Kepler's laws ==
{{Main|Kepler's laws of planetary motion}}
Newton showed that his law of gravitation mathematically implies [[Kepler's laws of planetary motion]], unifying terrestrial and celestial mechanics.

== Gravitational potential energy ==
The [[gravitational potential energy]] of two masses separated by distance <math>r</math> is:
:<math>U = -G\frac{m_1 m_2}{r}</math>

The negative sign indicates that work must be done to separate the masses against their gravitational attraction.

== Limits and Einstein's theory ==
{{Main|General relativity}}
Newton's law is an excellent approximation for most practical purposes but is superseded by [[Einstein]]'s theory of [[general relativity]], which describes gravitation as the curvature of [[spacetime]] caused by mass and energy.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Coulomb's law]]
* [[Gravitation]]
* [[Gravitational constant]]
* [[General relativity]]
* [[Kepler's laws of planetary motion]]
* [[Mass]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Gravitation]]
[[Category:Classical mechanics]]
[[Category:Scientific laws]]
[[Category:Isaac Newton]]
